meta.name: STENO Language Plover Dictionary
meta.description: Maps steno chords to STENO language tokens. Load above your main dictionary in Plover.

keyboard_map.note: This dictionary uses the standard steno layout as mapped to your QWERTY keyboard.
keyboard_map.steno_order: # S T K P W H R * A O - E U F R P B L G T S D Z

steno_token_chords.note: Each entry below maps a chord (in steno notation) to a STENO token string.

plover_setup.step_2: In Plover: Configure → Machine → set to 'Keyboard'
plover_setup.step_3: Copy this file to your Plover dictionaries folder
plover_setup.step_4: In Plover → Dictionaries: Add steno_lang_dict.json ABOVE your main dictionary
plover_setup.step_5: Click the green power button in Plover to enable
plover_setup.step_6: Now chords will output STENO tokens
plover_setup.keyboard_requirements: N-Key Rollover (NKRO) is required. Test at keyboardtester.com — press 10+ keys and verify all register simultaneously. Standard keyboards only support 6-key rollover.
plover_setup.nkro_qmk: If your keyboard uses QMK firmware: add NKRO_ENABLE = yes to rules.mk and #define FORCE_NKRO to config.h

chord_building_guide.note: Steno chords use simultaneous keypresses. Left hand = initial consonants, thumbs = vowels, right hand = final consonants.
chord_building_guide.number_bar: Any number key (1-0) adds # to the stroke
chord_building_guide.example: To type 'lp ' — press H, R, and - simultaneously (HR-P chord)
